title: Form Properties
description:
Form Properties

Form Properties and Events

The following list is a summary of the Form control properties with brief descriptions.  Click on the property names for more details.

Property	Description

AcceptButton	Gets or sets the button on the form
		 that is clicked when the user presses the ENTER key.

Default: (None)

BackColor	Selects the background color for the
		 object's label.

Default: Control

CancelButton	Gets or sets the button on the form
		 that is clicked when the user presses the ESC key.

Default: (None)

ControlBox	Gets or sets a value indicating whether
		 a control box is displayed in the caption bar of the form.

Default: 1 -
		 True

EventTaskType	Sets the type of task used for events.

Default: 0 -
		 Normal

FormBorderStyle	Gets or sets the border style of the
		 form.

Default: 3 -
		 FixedDialog

FormTemplate	Selects and sets the size of a form
		 from the template.

Height	Specifies the height of the control
		 in pixels.

HelpButton	Gets or sets a value indicating whether
		 a Help button should be displayed in the caption box of the form.

Default: 0 -
		 False

HelpID	Sets the ID for the help topic that
		 is displayed when the user clicks the title bar help button

Default: 0

Left	Sets the x
		 coordinate of the form in pixels.

Default:

MaximizeBox	Sets whether the title bar maximize
		 button is displayed.

Default: 0 -
		 False

MinimizeBox	Sets whether the title bar minimize
		 button is displayed.

Default: 0 -
		 False

Name	Sets the name of the control.

Default: FormXX

StartPosition	Gets or sets the starting position of
		 the form at run time.

Default: 1 -
		 CenterScreen

Text	Used to set the text of the control.

Default: Name
		 of form

Top	Sets the y coordinate of the form in
		 pixels.

Width	Sets the width
		 of the form in pixels.

WindowState	Sets the default window state for the
		 form.

Default: 0 -
		 Normal

Events	Description

Closed	Executes after the form is closed.

Load	Executes when the form loads.

Resize	Executes when the form is resized.
